Business Definition & Context: Unique identifier for the medical claim. (Excluded from training, used for tracking).
Unique identifier for the healthcare facility or physician submitting the claim.
Unique identifier for the patient receiving treatment.
The total gross dollar amount charged by the provider before any adjustments.
Standardized medical code (e.g., CPT/HCPCS) indicating the specific treatment administered.
Standardized medical code (e.g., ICD-10) indicating the patient's diagnosed condition.
The total number of days elapsed between the date of service and the claim submission date.
A flag (1 or 0) indicating if the billed amount exceeds a specific high-tier threshold.
A flag indicating if the patient has had prior claims within a trailing window.
The month the claim was submitted, capturing seasonal healthcare trends (e.g., flu season).
Historical historical approval rate of the provider, acting as a proxy score for trust.
The primary model target (e.g., Approved, Denied, Rejected).
The secondary target detailing the final resolution category of the claim.
